articles.title: BALANCER BLAMES ROUNDING ERROR FOR $128M MULTI-CHAIN EXPLOIT
articles.summary: Balancer has identified a rounding flaw in its swap logic as the root cause of the November 3 exploit that drained over $128 million from its Composable Stable Pools (CSPs) across multiple chains, including Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum, Avalanche, and Polygon. The issue stemmed from a rounding error in the upscale function for EXACT_OUT swaps within the v2 vault's batchSwap feature, which attackers used to manipulate pool balances and siphon liquidity. Emergency response teams like Hypernative, SEAL 911, StakeWise DAO, and BitFinding helped recover portions of the stolen funds, with StakeWise DAO retrieving 73.5% of osETH losses. The exploit primarily affected v5 CSPs, while v6 pools were automatically paused, and Balancer v3 remains unaffected.

articles.title: COINBASE URGES TREASURY TO KEEP GENIUS ACT INNOVATION-FRIENDLY
articles.summary: Coinbase has called on the US Treasury Department to ensure that upcoming regulations for the GENIUS Act remain faithful to congressional intent, warning against regulatory overreach that could stifle innovation and hinder America's ambition to become the “crypto capital of the world.” Chief Policy Officer Faryar Shirzad emphasized that the rules should focus narrowly on stablecoin issuers, not on software developers, validators, or open-source protocols. Coinbase also encouraged Treasury to clarify that loyalty and rewards programs do not constitute “interest” payments prohibited under the Act and to classify payment stablecoins as cash equivalents for tax and accounting purposes.

articles.title: ALMANAK'S AI STRATEGY BUILDER
articles.summary: Almanak's Strategy Builder allows agents to build strategies for users to deploy and dynamically change over time. Its launch video demoed an LP accumulation strategy to provide liquidity and accumulate the underlying tokens. Almanak's templates allow for slight alterations via natural language. Each strategy has a unique, AI-generated dashboard with custom metrics specific to the strategy's implementation.

articles.title: HYPERSTITION MARKETS
articles.summary: Hyperstition Markets introduce prediction markets engineered for reality construction rather than passive forecasting, rewarding traders for both accurate prediction and narrative enactment. The mechanism requires traders to demonstrate actions aligned with predictions verified through smart contracts, creating Nash equilibria where rational actors maximize returns by betting on and working toward specific futures. Applications include technological development bootstrapping, cultural transformation requiring epistemological framework shifts, and collective action problems where early believers profit from manifesting self-fulfilling prophecies. Unlike traditional prediction markets aiming for unbiased information aggregation, treating futures as discovered, Hyperstition Markets explicitly engineer reflexive loops between belief, incentive, and action, treating futures as designed.

articles.title: LIQUIDITY, THE LIFEBLOOD OF CRYPTO
articles.summary: Crypto's three primary liquidity channels – DATs, stablecoins, and ETFs – have plateaued after strong momentum through Q1 2025. Combined DATs and ETFs have grown from $40 billion to $270 billion since early 2024, while stablecoins doubled from $140 billion to $290 billion before flattening. Despite global M2 expansion and US quantitative tightening officially ending, high SOFR rates keep cash parked in T-bills yielding attractive returns, redirecting liquidity away from crypto into equity markets instead of compounding digital asset inflows. The slowdown across all three channels signals broad deceleration in fresh capital deployment rather than simple product rotation. Crypto entered a "self-funded phase" where capital rotates internally between majors and alt sectors rather than entering net new, creating PVP dynamics with short-lived rallies, narrowing breadth, and volatility spikes driven by liquidation cascades instead of sustained trends.

articles.title: ETHEREUM SHOULD TAKE THIS LESSON FROM HYPERLIQUID
APPS ARE NOW OUT-EARNING BLOCKCHAINS
articles.summary: Leading crypto applications like Pump.fun ($724M revenue) and Hyperliquid ($667M) are now out-earning the blockchains they're built on, such as Solana ($1.3B revenue, $632M in fees). These apps trade at far lower valuations despite using most of their revenue for token buybacks and transparent cash flows, while L1s retain only a fraction of the activity-generated value. The long-standing assumption that chains capture proportional value from ecosystem growth is breaking down as onchain value accrues to applications, not infrastructure. The “L1 premium” could continue eroding unless chains find new mechanisms, like white-labeled stablecoins, to internalize more of the liquidity and fees flowing through their ecosystems.

articles.title: ETHEREUM'S NEXT 5 YEARS AS THE RISK-FREE ASSET
articles.summary: Ethereum's future lies in proof aggregation, with L2s like ZKsync defragmenting the network and scaling it to millions of TPS, rendering alternative L1s obsolete. The Ethereum L1 remains the source of incorruptibility and neutrality, expected to reach 10,000+ TPS within three years, but still not enough for global demand. As L2s evolve into cashflow machines replacing traditional payment rails (e.g., credit cards, FX, and remittance apps), their success will flow back to Ethereum, which underpins their security. Investors should own the L2s generating yield to accumulate ETH, the risk-free asset of the EVM economy.

articles.title: ETH SEASON MAY BE FORMING AS FUNDAMENTALS OUTSHINE BTC
articles.summary: Ethereum's fundamentals now surpass Bitcoin's, making it the logical reserve asset for the onchain economy.

articles.title: HOW LIGHTER ACHIEVED 5,000+ TPS WITH <100MB/DAY ON ETHEREUM
articles.summary: Ethereum rollup Lighter hit a record 5,000+ TPS while posting under 100MB/day to Ethereum, compared to Base's 150 TPS and 2GB/day.

articles.title: ETHEREUM FUNDAMENTALS STRONGER THAN EVER
articles.summary: Since ETH last traded at $4,600, we've seen record-breaking L1 and L2 transaction volumes, JPMorgan's plan to accept ETH as collateral, SEC approval of staked ETH ETFs, and the rise of tokenized assets like gold and Fidelity's $200M onchain fund.

articles.title: LUCID AGENTS
articles.summary: Lucid Agents is a developer library by the Daydreams team that combines x402 and ERC-8004 together to create trustless, verifiable agents that can pay one another and monetize their services.
